project.name: Module 14: Hush 1.0 Import (SJCL)
project.description: Add 'Import from Hush' feature — SJCL-based decryption of original Hush extension export blobs. Lazy-loads SJCL (~50KB) via dynamic import, decrypts the self-describing JSON blob using the user's original Hush password, maps the Hush data model (folders/bookmarks) to Hush's BookmarkTree format, and returns the tree + stats to the caller (persistence handled by existing SAVE flow). New message type IMPORT_HUSH added to background protocol. UI added to existing ImportSection in settings. This is the final migration path for Hush's ~10K remaining users — key GTM differentiator.
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 5 (Hush import)
project.critical_path_position: Module 14 is independent. No upstream dependency beyond Modules 1-13 (all complete). No downstream modules depend on Module 14.

do_not_incorporate: Holy PB has no Hush import capability — this is entirely new functionality
Do NOT use SJCL for any Hush-native encryption — SJCL is import-only
build_from_scratch: lib/hush-import.ts: SJCL decryption of Hush export blob, data model mapping (Hush folders/bookmarks → BookmarkTree), import stats generation. Pure TypeScript, no UI. SJCL loaded via dynamic import().
IMPORT_HUSH message type added to lib/background-types.ts
HushImportSection component in components/settings/ — textarea for blob paste, password field, import button, stats display
Hush data model types: HushExportData, HushFolder, HushBookmark (internal to hush-import.ts, not exported to lib/types.ts)

user_stories.title: lib/hush-import.ts — SJCL decryption and data model mapping
user_stories.description: Create lib/hush-import.ts with: (1) decryptHushBlob(blob: string, password: string) → decrypts SJCL JSON blob using raw password, returns parsed HushExportData. (2) mapHushToTree(data: HushExportData) → maps Hush folders/bookmarks to BookmarkNode[] suitable for appending to existing tree. (3) importHushData(blob: string, password: string) → full pipeline: decrypt → validate → map → wrap in 'Hush Import' folder → return Result<{ tree, stats }, ImportError | InvalidPasswordError>. SJCL is lazy-loaded via dynamic import('sjcl'). Hush export format is self-describing JSON: {iv, v, iter, ks, ts, mode, adata, cipher, salt, ct}. NOTE: dynamic import() is a new pattern for lib/ — no existing module uses it. This is intentional for lazy-loading heavy dependencies.
steps: PREREQUISITE: Install sjcl as dependency: npm install sjcl --legacy-peer-deps. Check if @types/sjcl exists on npm; if not, create a minimal type declaration file (e.g., types/sjcl.d.ts). The only function needed is sjcl.decrypt(password: string, ciphertext: string): string.

RED: Test: 'decryptHushBlob decrypts valid SJCL blob with correct password' — NOTE: sjcl can be imported at top-level in test files (lazy-load constraint is production-only). Use sjcl.encrypt() in test setup to generate valid fixtures.
RED: Test: 'decryptHushBlob throws InvalidPasswordError for wrong password'
RED: Test: 'decryptHushBlob throws ImportError for malformed JSON blob'
RED: Test: 'mapHushToTree maps folders and bookmarks to BookmarkNode[]'
RED: Test: 'mapHushToTree filters out Trash folder'
RED: Test: 'mapHushToTree handles empty folders gracefully'
RED: Test: 'mapHushToTree maps Hush bookmark fields (text→title, url→url, created→dateAdded)'
RED: Test: 'mapHushToTree handles pre-1.0 exports without folders array (wraps flat content in default folder)'
RED: Test: 'importHushData wraps import in Hush Import folder and returns Result with stats'
RED: Test: 'importHushData returns Result with ImportStats (folder/bookmark counts)'
RED: Test: 'importHushData returns error Result for wrong password (InvalidPasswordError)'

GREEN: Create lib/hush-import.ts. Define internal types HushExportData = { key?: string; folders: HushFolder[] }, HushFolder = { id: string; title: string; bookmarks: HushBookmark[] }, HushBookmark = { url: string; text: string; created: string }. Implement decryptHushBlob using dynamic import('sjcl') — throws InvalidPasswordError for wrong password, ImportError for format errors. Implement mapHushToTree — filter Trash, handle missing folders array (pre-1.0 edge case). Implement importHushData returning Result<{ tree: BookmarkTree; stats: ImportStats }, ImportError | InvalidPasswordError>. Silently ignore the 'key' field in HushExportData (no logging).
REFACTOR: Ensure SJCL is ONLY imported inside decryptHushBlob (lazy-load). Verify no SJCL types leak into lib/types.ts. Verify InvalidPasswordError for password errors, ImportError for format errors.
acceptance_criteria: lib/hush-import.ts exports: decryptHushBlob, mapHushToTree, importHushData
SJCL loaded via dynamic import('sjcl') ONLY inside decryptHushBlob — never at module top level
decryptHushBlob: takes (blob: string, password: string), returns decrypted+parsed HushExportData
decryptHushBlob: wrong password → InvalidPasswordError (not ImportError)
decryptHushBlob: malformed blob → ImportError with source: 'hush'
mapHushToTree: filters out Trash folder (title === 'Trash')
mapHushToTree: maps text→title, url→url, created→dateAdded (ISO string → timestamp)
mapHushToTree: generates unique IDs for each folder and bookmark (via generateId())
mapHushToTree: handles pre-1.0 exports without folders array (wraps flat content in default folder)
importHushData: returns Result<{ tree: BookmarkTree; stats: ImportStats }, ImportError | InvalidPasswordError>
importHushData: wraps mapped nodes in 'Hush Import' folder
importHushData: stats include folder count, bookmark count, errors array
Hush 'key' field silently ignored — zero console.log
Zero SJCL types or references in lib/types.ts
definition_of_done: SJCL not in top-level imports of hush-import.ts

user_stories.title: IMPORT_HUSH message type and background handler
user_stories.description: Add IMPORT_HUSH to the background message protocol (17th type). Wire handler in background/handlers.ts: receive blob + password → importHushData → return tree + stats to caller. Handler does NOT write to storage — persistence is handled by the existing SAVE message flow (matching handleImportChromeBookmarks and handleImportBackup patterns). Update background-types.ts union. Update exhaustive switch.
steps: RED: Test: 'IMPORT_HUSH handler calls importHushData and returns tree + stats on success'
RED: Test: 'IMPORT_HUSH handler returns INVALID_PASSWORD code for wrong password (instanceof InvalidPasswordError check)'
RED: Test: 'IMPORT_HUSH handler returns IMPORT_ERROR code for format failure'

GREEN: Add ImportHushMessage = { type: 'IMPORT_HUSH'; blob: string; password: string } to background-types.ts. Add to BackgroundMessage union (now 17 types). Update MessageType. Wire handler: importHushData(msg.blob, msg.password) → if (!result.success) check instanceof InvalidPasswordError → code: 'INVALID_PASSWORD', else code: 'IMPORT_ERROR' → if success, return { tree: result.data.tree, stats: result.data.stats }. Match handleImportBackup pattern at handlers.ts:273-282. Update switch in index.ts.
REFACTOR: Update scaffold-smoke.test.ts LIB_MODULES count (14, now includes hush-import.ts). Verify exhaustive switch still compiles.
acceptance_criteria: background-types.ts: ImportHushMessage type added, BackgroundMessage union now 17 types
MessageType includes 'IMPORT_HUSH'
Handler does NOT require unlocked session (Hush import uses its own password, not the active password)
Handler calls importHushData(msg.blob, msg.password)
Handler checks instanceof InvalidPasswordError → returns { success: false, error: 'Invalid password', code: 'INVALID_PASSWORD' }
Handler returns { success: true, data: { tree, stats } } on success
Handler does NOT write to any storage — returns tree to caller (persistence via SAVE flow)
Exhaustive switch compiles cleanly (no missing case)
LIB_MODULES count updated to 14 in scaffold-smoke

user_stories.title: HushImportSection UI component
user_stories.description: Create HushImportSection component in components/settings/ — textarea for pasting Hush export blob, password field (PasswordInput), import button with loading state, success stats display, error display. Wired into SettingsScreen within the existing Import/Export section. Sends IMPORT_HUSH message via useSendMessage. Follows established wait-for-response pattern from ImportSection.

user_stories.title: E2E Hush import flows and integration verification
user_stories.description: E2E tests for the full Hush import flow. Generate a real SJCL-encrypted test fixture (encrypt a known Hush data structure with a known password using SJCL in the test setup — top-level import OK in test files). Verify: paste blob → enter password → import → bookmarks appear in tree. Verify error paths: wrong password, malformed blob. Full verification suite.
steps: Create SJCL test fixture: use sjcl.encrypt() in test setup to create a valid encrypted blob from a known HushExportData. This ensures the test fixture is always valid. NOTE: sjcl top-level import is OK in test files — the lazy-load constraint is production-only.
RED: E2E: 'Hush import: paste valid blob + correct password → bookmarks appear'
RED: E2E: 'Hush import: wrong password → shows error'
RED: E2E: 'Hush import: malformed blob → shows error'
RED: E2E: 'Hush import: empty textarea → button disabled'
GREEN: Create tests/e2e/hush-import.test.ts with fixture generation and flow tests.
Run full verification suite: tsc + eslint + vitest + wxt build + playwright
acceptance_criteria: E2E: valid import flow creates bookmarks in tree (excluding Trash)
E2E: wrong password shows user-friendly error (not crypto internals)
E2E: malformed blob shows error
E2E: empty fields disable button
Full verification passes: tsc + eslint + vitest + wxt build + playwright
Zero regressions in existing tests

what_exists.lib_modules: 13 files in lib/: types.ts, errors.ts, sentry.ts, utils.ts, crypto.ts, storage.ts, data-model.ts, bookmark-import.ts, bookmark-backup.ts, password-sets.ts, recovery.ts, incognito.ts, background-types.ts. scaffold-smoke tracks 13 LIB_MODULES (background-types is counted as lib/).
what_exists.background: entrypoints/background/index.ts + handlers.ts. ALL 16 handlers implemented. Zero NOT_IMPLEMENTED. HandlerContext for dependency injection.
what_exists.background_types: lib/background-types.ts: 16-type BackgroundMessage union (Unlock, Lock, Save, GetState, AddBookmark, GetIncognitoState, ChangePassword, UpdateAutoLock, CreateSet, RenameSet, DeleteSet, SwitchSet, ClearAll, ImportChromeBookmarks, ImportBackup, ExportBackup). BackgroundResponse with success/error/code. SessionState with hasData: boolean.
what_exists.import_section: components/settings/ImportSection.tsx: 3 import flows (Chrome bookmarks, HTML file, encrypted backup). Does NOT have Hush import.
what_exists.settings_screen: components/screens/SettingsScreen.tsx: Account, Data Management (Import/Export), Preferences, Danger Zone sections.
what_exists.result_type: lib/types.ts exports Result<T, E> = { success: true; data: T } | { success: false; error: E }. Used by all import functions.
what_exists.error_classes: lib/errors.ts: InvalidPasswordError (password failures), ImportError (format/structural failures with context: { source?, format? }). Handler pattern: check instanceof InvalidPasswordError → code: 'INVALID_PASSWORD', else code: 'IMPORT_ERROR'.
what_exists.handler_pattern: Import handlers (handleImportChromeBookmarks, handleImportBackup) return tree + stats to caller. They do NOT write to storage — persistence is handled by the SAVE message flow.

key_exports.data_model: addBookmark, createEmptyTree, isBookmark, isFolder, generateId, MAX_TREE_DEPTH

what_does_not_exist.hush_import: lib/hush-import.ts does not exist. No SJCL dependency. No IMPORT_HUSH message type.
what_does_not_exist.sjcl: sjcl package not installed. No type declarations for sjcl.
what_does_not_exist.hush_import_ui: No HushImportSection component. ImportSection only handles Chrome/HTML/backup.
what_does_not_exist.dynamic_imports: No existing lib/ module uses dynamic import(). This module introduces the pattern for lazy-loading heavy dependencies.

tech_stack.sjcl: Stanford Javascript Crypto Library. Install via npm install sjcl --legacy-peer-deps. Lazy-load via dynamic import('sjcl') in production code — NEVER top-level import in lib/. Top-level import OK in test files. ~50KB.
tech_stack.sjcl_types: If @types/sjcl does not exist on npm, create minimal declaration: declare module 'sjcl' { export function decrypt(password: string, ciphertext: string): string; export function encrypt(password: string, plaintext: string): string; }. Place in a .d.ts file (e.g., types/sjcl.d.ts).
tech_stack.hush_export_format: Self-describing JSON: {iv:<base64>, v:1, iter:10000, ks:128, ts:64, mode:'ccm', adata:'', cipher:'aes', salt:<base64>, ct:<base64>}. sjcl.decrypt() handles all params automatically from the blob.
tech_stack.hush_key_handling: Export uses RAW password (not hashed). sjcl.decrypt(password, blob) with raw password. No need to implement hashKey() fallback for exports — only local storage used hashed keys.
tech_stack.hush_data_model: Decrypted JSON: { key?: string, folders: [{ id: string, title: string, bookmarks: [{ url: string, text: string, created: string }] }] }. Standard folders: 'Home' and 'Trash'. Filter out Trash on import. Silently ignore the 'key' field (no logging — zero console.log per CLAUDE.md).
tech_stack.pre_1_0_edge_case: Very old Hush exports (pre-1.0) may lack a folders array. Handle gracefully: if no folders array, treat entire decrypted content as flat bookmark list wrapped in a default folder.
tech_stack.testing: Vitest + happy-dom for unit. Playwright for E2E. For test fixtures: generate real SJCL blobs using sjcl.encrypt() at top-level import in test files (lazy-load constraint is production-only).
forbidden_patterns: Top-level import of SJCL in production code (lib/, components/, entrypoints/)
Using SJCL for any Hush-native encryption
Importing crypto.ts functions for Hush import (SJCL handles its own crypto)
Throwing raw errors — use InvalidPasswordError or ImportError
Returning raw values — use Result<T, E> pattern

key_facts: Hush v1.3.0 uses SJCL with AES-128-CCM, PBKDF2-SHA256, 10K iterations
Export format is self-describing JSON — all crypto params embedded
sjcl.decrypt(password, blob) handles everything given correct password
Decrypted plaintext is JSON.stringify'd data — parse after decrypt
Recommendation: Option C (Hybrid) — SJCL for import only, Web Crypto for native

read_first: Hush_Encryption_Analysis.docx (in project knowledge — critical reference for data format)
lib/bookmark-import.ts (pattern reference: ImportResult, ImportStats, convertChromeBookmarks for tree mapping)
lib/bookmark-backup.ts (pattern reference: importEncryptedBackup for Result + InvalidPasswordError pattern)
components/settings/ImportSection.tsx (pattern reference: existing import UI flows)

inputs.what_it_provides: Bookmark, Folder, BookmarkNode, BookmarkTree, ImportStats (via bookmark-import.ts), Result<T, E>
HandlerContext, existing handler patterns (handleImportBackup at line 273)

outputs.description: Hush SJCL decryption + data mapping, returns Result<T,E>

known_risks.issue: SJCL npm package may not have TypeScript types
known_risks.action: Create minimal .d.ts declaring sjcl.decrypt() and sjcl.encrypt(). Only two functions needed.
Acceptable. Used only for import (read), not for new encryption. The format is frozen.
sjcl.decrypt throws on invalid JSON or failed auth tag. Catch and return ImportError Result (not throw).

known_risks.issue: Very old Hush exports (pre-1.0) may lack folders
known_risks.action: Handle in mapHushToTree: if no folders array, wrap flat content in default folder.
Lazy-loaded via dynamic import(). Only loaded when user triggers Hush import. Not in main bundle.

known_risks.issue: Hush export includes plaintext password in encrypted blob (key field)
known_risks.action: Silently ignore the key field during mapping. Do not store, display, or log it. Zero console.log per CLAUDE.md.

known_risks.issue: dynamic import() is a new pattern for lib/
known_risks.action: Intentional for lazy-loading heavy deps. Works with Vite bundler. No existing lib/ module needs to change.

dependency_chain.downstream: None. Module 14 is a leaf — no other modules depend on it.
dependency_chain.parallel_safe: Can run in parallel with Module 15a or 16a if needed.

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session. Research sjcl npm package, verify decrypt() API, check TypeScript types availability. Commit per story.
metadata.quality_gate: All tests pass. tsc clean. wxt build. SJCL lazy-loaded only. Zero SJCL in lib/types.ts. InvalidPasswordError for password failures. ImportError for format failures. Result<T,E> pattern for all returns. Password cleared from state on success.
metadata.human_review_required: Verify: SJCL decrypt works with real Hush export blob (if available), Trash folder filtered, password field cleared, no PII in errors.

execution_rules.clarifying_questions: Encouraged. Key: sjcl npm package API, type declaration approach, test fixture generation strategy.
execution_rules.prd_scope: Module 14 of 22. Last module with holy_pb_shortfalls section.
